evals.prompt: Add a password reset feature to this auth service using TDD
evals.expected_output: Claude plans the interface and behaviors first, writes one failing test (tracer bullet), implements minimal code to pass it, then continues one test at a time. Does NOT write all tests upfront before implementing.

assertions.name: plans-before-coding
assertions.description: Claude confirms interface design and which behaviors to test before writing any test or implementation code

assertions.name: tracer-bullet-first
assertions.description: The first test written is a single focused test for one behavior — not a batch of tests

assertions.name: no-horizontal-slicing
assertions.description: Claude does NOT write all tests upfront before any implementation (anti-pattern avoided)

assertions.name: vertical-cycles
assertions.description: Each test is followed by its implementation before the next test is written

assertions.name: uses-public-interface
assertions.description: Tests verify behavior through the public API, not internal state or direct DB queries

evals.prompt: Write tests as we build this new cart checkout flow
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers on loose phrasing. Claude adopts TDD approach — confirms behaviors to test, proposes a tracer bullet, proceeds incrementally.

assertions.name: tdd-approach-adopted
assertions.description: Claude adopts a structured TDD approach even though the user didn't say 'TDD' or 'red-green-refactor'

assertions.name: confirms-behaviors
assertions.description: Claude asks about or explicitly proposes which behaviors to test before starting any code

assertions.name: incremental-cycles
assertions.description: Work proceeds one test → one implementation at a time rather than all at once

evals.prompt: I want to do red-green-refactor on this payments module — start with the charge() method
evals.expected_output: Claude focuses on the charge() method, writes one failing test, implements minimal code to pass, then checks for refactor candidates. Does not over-plan or expand scope.

assertions.name: stays-scoped-to-charge
assertions.description: Claude stays focused on charge() and does not expand scope to other methods unprompted

assertions.name: single-tracer-bullet
assertions.description: Starts with one test for one behavior of charge() rather than multiple tests at once

assertions.name: refactor-step-present
assertions.description: After reaching GREEN, Claude explicitly checks for refactor candidates

assertions.name: minimal-green-code
assertions.description: Implementation at the GREEN step is minimal — no speculative or anticipatory code added
